meta.voice: Brendan Franks — data-first, fragments, contractions, no em-dashes, no links in main tweet
meta.rules: Under 280 chars. Lead with a number where possible. Fragments for emphasis. Link in the reply only. Mentions go in a SECOND reply (third tweet) — keeps the main thread clean and doesn't spam mentions in body copy.

posts.text:
Buckeye's own engineering report said a roundabout would cut injury crashes 41.7% at Durango and Yuma.

The City picked the cheaper signalized design. 15.4% reduction.

26.3-point safety gap. Saved $1.4M up front. Built the difference into the next decade of crash reports.
posts.reply:
Full investigation with the CMF table, FHWA benchmarks, and the population-growth math:

azlawnow.com/investigations/buckeye-durango-yuma-roundabout-rejected/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @azfamily @fox10phoenix @12News @abc15 @KJZZPhoenix — Buckeye infrastructure / West Valley desks if useful.

posts.text:
FHWA data on signalized-to-roundabout conversions:

46% reduction in fatal and serious injury crashes.

Buckeye had this in front of them at Durango and Yuma. Picked the cheaper option anyway.

The math doesn't care about public unfamiliarity.
posts.reply:
We pulled the Crash Modification Factor studies, the City's evaluation matrix, and the cost line:

azlawnow.com/investigations/buckeye-durango-yuma-roundabout-rejected/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @AZMirror @azcapitoltimes for the local-government accountability angle.

posts.text:
60% of nursing home sexual abuse victims have dementia.

80% of perpetrators are caregivers.

Only 30% of incidents reach law enforcement.

A Mesa caregiver was arrested yesterday. Wheelchair-bound dementia patient. The family caught it on video.
posts.reply:
The case fits the pattern. HB2228 is moving in the AZ Senate to close the registry gap. Full piece with primary sources:

azlawnow.com/investigations/grand-court-mesa-elder-abuse-hb2228/
posts.mentions: cc @AARPArizona @azcentral @AZMirror @azfamily @fox10phoenix @12News @abc15 @cronkitenews — elder care + AZ legislature beats.

posts.text:
1,816 sexual abuse complaints in long-term care facilities in 2024 alone.

60% jump from 2017.

Nearly half of victims die within a year of the assault.

This isn't an edge case. It's a sector.
posts.reply:
Yesterday's Mesa arrest is one of them. AZ HB2228 is the structural fix moving in the Senate this week:

azlawnow.com/investigations/grand-court-mesa-elder-abuse-hb2228/
posts.mentions: cc @AARP @AARPArizona @JusticeInAging @LTCCC — national + state long-term care advocacy.

posts.text:
Buckeye, Arizona. 2020 population: 93,741.

2024 estimate: 114,334.

32.95% growth in four years. Top tier of fastest-growing US cities.

More people. More AADT. Same arterial intersections. The road geometry decisions made today carry for decades.
posts.reply:
Why the City's choice at Durango and Yuma compounds:

azlawnow.com/investigations/buckeye-durango-yuma-roundabout-rejected/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @AZMirror @azcapitoltimes @ASUCronkite — fastest-growing-cities + planning beats.

posts.text:
If your loved one is in an Arizona long-term care facility:

1. Pull the AZDHS inspection record.
2. Document anything that doesn't sit right.
3. APS hotline: 1-877-767-2385.

The data on what happens when families don't is the reason HB2228 exists.
posts.reply:
Full investigation with the statutory references and reporting paths:

azlawnow.com/investigations/grand-court-mesa-elder-abuse-hb2228/
posts.mentions: cc @AARPArizona @AZHealthDept @AZAdultProtSvcs — facility oversight + APS reporting.

posts.text:
Arizona gives you 180 days to file a Notice of Claim against a public entity.

Tempe admits pavement near ASU is in poor condition.

And the Arizona Board of Regents says ASU isn't authorized to accept your paperwork.

That's the trap.
posts.reply:
Full investigation with ARS 12-821.01 verbatim, Fong v. Phoenix (2024), the ABOR service rule, and the jurisdictional map:

azlawnow.com/investigations/tempe-asu-pavement-180-day-claim-clock/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @cronkitenews @statepress @KJZZPhoenix @fox10phoenix, ASU + Tempe infrastructure beats.

posts.text:
Fong v. City of Phoenix, 2024.

A cyclist rode into an unbarricaded ditch in an active bike-lane excavation.

Arizona Court of Appeals affirmed summary judgment for the city.

Expert testimony required to get past the public-entity defense on pavement cases.
posts.reply:
What that ruling does to a scooter or bike crash near ASU, plus the 180-day Notice of Claim clock and the ABOR service rule:

azlawnow.com/investigations/tempe-asu-pavement-180-day-claim-clock/
posts.mentions: cc @azcourts @azcentral @AZMirror @statepress, bicycle safety + municipal liability beats.

posts.text:
Arizona licensed 237 private career schools in FY25.

37 got hit with adverse actions.

Nine more closed.

One fiscal year. 15.6% action rate. That's not a rounding error.
posts.reply:
Why the federal 60% rule under 34 CFR 668.22 structurally explains the pattern, with Corinthian and ITT precedent and AZ tort theories:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-career-schools-37-adverse-actions/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @AZMirror @cronkitenews @PhoenixNewTimes @highereddive, higher ed + consumer protection beats.

posts.text:
Department of Education discharged $5.8B for 560,000 Corinthian borrowers.

$3.9B for 208,000 ITT borrowers.

Both in 2022. Both on misrepresentation findings.

Arizona's Private Postsecondary Board issued 37 adverse actions of its own in FY25.
posts.reply:
The structural frame, the federal rule that ties funding to enrollment thresholds, and the AZ tort theory under Pruitt v. Pavelin:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-career-schools-37-adverse-actions/
posts.mentions: cc @usedgov @BorrowerDefense @tCAPP @defendstudents, for-profit college accountability.

posts.text:
ASHRAE 241, the post-pandemic standard, says it in plain English:

Air that goes around an unsealed filter doesn't get cleaned. The system performs as if a lower MERV had been installed.

Arizona schools bought hospital-grade MERV 13 filters. Most installs are drop-ins. The housings aren't sealed.
posts.reply:
Full investigation with the ASHRAE 52.2 + 241 + 62.1 frame, EPA's 9 µg/m³ PM2.5 NAAQS, peer-reviewed PM2.5 health data, AZ tort theory, and the PRR templates parents can file:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-schools-merv-13-filter-bypass/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @cronkitenews @AZMirror @ashraehq @EPA, school IAQ + ventilation accountability.

posts.text:
EPA tightened the annual PM2.5 standard to 9 µg/m³ in 2024.

Di et al. 2017, NEJM: 7.3% mortality jump per µg/m³ in Medicare cohort.

No Arizona school district publishes a single PM2.5 reading from inside its own buildings.

That absence is the headline.
posts.reply:
What the published peer-reviewed record says about PM2.5 and child cognition, plus Arizona's 180-day Notice of Claim clock for school district claims:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-schools-merv-13-filter-bypass/
posts.mentions: cc @EPAregion9 @AZHealthDept @AZSchoolBoards @AZPTAdvocacy, school IAQ accountability.

posts.text:
Arizona disciplined 325 educators in 2024.

That's a 491% increase from 2012.

Sexual misconduct accounts for 36% of every case the Board has ruled on since 2012. 671 of 1,876.

Maricopa County drove 55.6% of the pipeline.
posts.reply:
Full investigation: the Board's own 2024 Enforcement Action Report, the 12-year category breakdown, the geography, plus what survivor families need to know about the ARS 12-514 and ARS 12-821.01 clocks:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-325-educator-discipline-2024/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @AZMirror @cronkitenews @PhoenixNewTimes @AZCIR @ABC15Arizona, education accountability beat.

posts.text:
Voluntary surrender is the dominant outcome in Arizona educator misconduct cases. 39% across 13 years. 2024 set an annual high.

When a parent hears an educator 'voluntarily resigned,' it often means a Board surrender that ends Arizona certification.

The public discipline portal is the verification tool.
posts.reply:
How the Board's process works, what voluntary surrender actually means for a survivor's record, and the search portal at azsbe.az.gov/educator-discipline/discipline-search:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-325-educator-discipline-2024/
posts.mentions: cc @AZStateBoardEd @AZSchoolBoards @ACESDV @RAINN, parent advocacy + survivor support.

posts.text:
Arizona's AG sued the federal government Friday to block a 1,500-bed detention facility in Surprise.

Across the street: Rinchem Company stores chlorine, hydrofluoric acid, and fluorine.

Rinchem's EPA Risk Management Plan was filed Jan 1, 2026. Three weeks before the federal purchase.
posts.reply:
Full investigation: the Mullin docket, the Rinchem RMP frame, NEPA + INA + Clean Air Act claims, and the Maryland TRO precedent (March 11, 2026):

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-mayes-ice-surprise-detention-lawsuit/
posts.mentions: cc @ag_mayes @AZMirror @cronkitenews @KJZZPhoenix @azcentral @PhoenixNewTimes, federal accountability + immigration + NEPA beats.

posts.text:
Three sister-state AGs already filed materially similar NEPA suits in 2026:

Maryland March 19 (TRO won March 11).
New Jersey March 20.
Michigan March 24.

Arizona made it four on April 24. The procedural argument has worked.
posts.reply:
The Eleventh Circuit's 'more than minimal federal funding and control' test post-Florida, Arizona's $70M federal purchase + $300M operations contracts, and what comes next:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-mayes-ice-surprise-detention-lawsuit/
posts.mentions: cc @ag_mayes @StateImpactCenter @CenterForBioDiv, NEPA + state-AG-coordination beats.

posts.text:
ASU Preparatory Academy posted a $5.46M operating loss on $82.4M revenue last fiscal year.

Its FY21 audit discloses a related-party lease on a school-site building. $11.5M right-of-use asset. Agreement runs through September 2032.

The named counterparty is in Note 9. We're pulling it.
posts.reply:
Full breakdown: the audit text, the federal-funds schedule, the ALA precedent, and the public-records path under ARS 39-121:

azlawnow.com/investigations/asu-prep-related-party-lease/
posts.mentions: cc @azcentral @AZMirror @azcapitoltimes @CronkiteNews — charter accountability / education finance desks if useful.

posts.text:
Arizona's largest university-affiliated charter network leases its school building from a related party.

The FY21 audit's Note 6 says it. Note 9 names the party. The lease runs to 2032.

This is on the public record at ade.az.gov. Anyone can pull it.
posts.reply:

azlawnow.com/investigations/asu-prep-related-party-lease/

posts.text:
Walgreens settled with the federal government for up to $350 million in April 2025.

The allegation: knowingly filling unlawful opioid prescriptions, August 2012 through March 2023.

Arizona Walgreens stores aren't enumerated in publicly retrievable secondary press.

They are in the consent decree exhibits.

posts.text:
The APS / Korman $7M settlement reinstates the 95-degree heat hold. ACC Commissioner Nick Myers, on record at @KJZZPhoenix: "I refuse to tell utilities they have to provide power to people that do not pay their bills."

Three days. 99 degrees. Six days later, dead.

We have the AG release, the ME findings, and the named ACC vote breakdown. Dossier is publication-ready. Reach out for the records.
